WARNING: These fault patterns are for A2779 STANDARD (820-02841, X2523/MLB_S) ONLY. Component designators are board-specific. No A2779_S-specific repair data exists yet — these patterns are PREDICTED from A2681/A2442 data and M2 Pro architecture analysis. Update this file as real repair data comes in.
description: Predicted fault patterns based on A2681/A2442 diagnostic data adapted for M2 Pro architecture. M2 Pro has more power stages (Austringer, 3 CLVRs), more USB-C ports, and additional peripherals (HDMI, SD card) creating additional fault vectors.
data_source: PREDICTED from A2681 Safan data + A2442 architecture analysis. No confirmed A2779_S repair records yet.

faults.name: CD3217 (UF400/UF500/UF600) failure
faults.predicted_frequency: High — same CD3217 IC as A2681, 3 instances vs 2

symptoms.dcps_5v: 0A or trickle (single port), 0A (all ports if liquid damage)
symptoms.dcps_20v: Trickle or normal (if only one port affected)

root_causes: Previous repair with wrong CD3217 variant
faults.fix: Replace CD3217 (UF400, UF500, or UF600). Ensure matching ROM. 3 ports = test each individually to isolate.

faults.name: PP3V8_AON short (VDDMAIN / distribution)
faults.predicted_frequency: High — same U5700 SVR AON architecture as A2681
root_causes: Willamette WiFi/BT module pulling PP3V8_AON_WLBT to ground
faults.fix: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON distribution. Thermal cam to find hot component. Common on VDDMAIN branch.

faults.name: PPBUS_AON short
root_causes: Liquid damage on main bus trace
faults.fix: Diode mode on PPBUS_AON. Find shorted cap. Remove and retest. Check J5150/U5150 SYS_DETECT circuit.

faults.name: U5700 (SVR AON) failure
faults.predicted_frequency: Medium — 30A 3-phase buck, same architecture as A2681
root_causes: Downstream short causing U5700 overcurrent shutdown
faults.fix: Check PPVAON_PWR_EN from J5150. Check 3V8 enable delay circuit (page 30 — DELAY FOR 3.8V VR ENABLE / BYPASSED). If enable present and no output → U5700 dead.

faults.name: U8100 (MPMU) failure
root_causes: Liquid damage on MPMU BGA pads
faults.fix: Check AON LDOs (PP1V8_AON_MPMU, PP1V2_AON_MPMU). If multiple outputs missing → replace U8100. If single output → find downstream short.

faults.name: U7700 (SPMU) failure — Note BUCK 7 NC on S variant
faults.note: SPMU Buck 7 is NC on S (Standard) variant. Do NOT expect output. Do NOT confuse with a fault.
faults.fix: Check AON LDOs (PP1V8_AON_SPMU, PP1V2_AON_SPMU). If missing → replace U7700.

faults.name: Austringer (U9300) failure
faults.predicted_frequency: Low-Medium — new IC type not on A2681

symptoms.dcps_20v: 0.3-1.0A (partial boot, SoC domains not fully powered)

faults.fix: Check FIVSDOH_FAULT_L and FIVSDOH_SMOS_FAULT_L. Check Austringer power stage caps (pages 49-50). If controller dead → replace U9300.

faults.name: CLVR instance failure (UA100/UA200/UA600)
faults.predicted_frequency: Low-Medium — 3 instances, S variant specific CLVR2 config

symptoms.dcps_20v: 0.5-1.5A (partial boot, specific SoC domain not powered)

root_causes: Address strap misconfiguration (S vs C/D variant mixup)
faults.note: CLVR2 (UA600) address straps and CFG are DIFFERENT between S and C/D variants. S=CFG C (6+1+13+6), OTP=IP. Do NOT install C variant CLVR2 on S board.
faults.fix: Check each CLVR output independently. Verify address straps match S variant (page 54). Check PP1V8_S1_CLVR_VDDH input.

faults.name: SSD / NAND power fault (dual SSD)
faults.note: A2779_S has DUAL SSD — SSD0 (pages 100-104) and SSD1 (pages 105-109). Check both. NAND CLK test points on page 110.
faults.fix: Check NAND power rails for both SSDs. Check for boot loop pattern.

faults.name: Liquid damage — multiple component failure
faults.predicted_frequency: High — most common overall fault category
faults.fix: Clean board thoroughly (IPA + ultrasonic). Fix one short at a time, retest after each. A2779_S has more components than A2681 = more potential liquid damage points.
common_areas: CD3217 area (UF400/UF500/UF600) — USB-C controllers near edges

faults.name: SYS_DETECT / BMU (J5150/U5150) fault

symptoms.dcps_5v: Trickle or no current (PPBUS_AON doesn't come up)

root_causes: U5150 (BLKAMPMHY) IC failure — marked CRITICAL in schematic
faults.fix: Check J5150 connector pins. Check U5150 (BLKAMPMHY CRITICAL). Verify PPVAON_PWR_EN signal path from J5150 to U5700.

faults.name: Display / backlight fault (Luxe UP800)
faults.affected_rail: Display power sequencer outputs, Luxe backlight
faults.note: Luxe UP800 replaces BEN (U6560) from A2681 — different IC, different pinout. CP800/CP801 MUST BE MIRRORED. CP834/CP835 MUST BE MIRRORED.
faults.fix: Check display sequencer (UP710/UP730/UP701). Check Luxe (UP800). Test with external display via HDMI (JH500 page 89) to isolate panel vs GPU.
